Title: BBX16, a B-box protein, positively regulates light-induced anthocyanin accumulation by activating PpMYB10 and anthocyanin structural genes in red pear

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B-box (BBX) zinc finger protein; homolog of Arabidopsis BBX22
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel; also induced in pear calli, Arabidopsis hypocotyls and tops of flower stalks in heterologous assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively promotes light-induced anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpBBX16 was rapidly induced by white light in bag-removed red pear peel before major induction of most anthocyanin structural genes. 2. Ectopic expression of PpBBX16 in Arabidopsis increased anthocyanin accumulation and upregulated anthocyanin pathway genes. 3. Overexpression in pear calli and transient overexpression in pear peel enhanced light-induced anthocyanin accumulation, whereas VIGS silencing reduced anthocyanin accumulation. 4. PpBBX16 localized to the nucleus and had trans-activation activity. 5. PpBBX16 did not directly bind PpMYB10 or PpCHS promoters in yeast one-hybrid assays, but interacted with PpHY5 and together strongly activated PpMYB10 and PpCHS promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Light-induction expression analysis; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; pear callus overexpression; transient overexpression in pear fruit peel; VIGS silencing in pear fruit; subcellular localization; yeast transactivation assay; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; anthocyanin quantification; RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by white light; upregulated within 6 h after light exposure in bag-removed fruit peel
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by white light in pear peel; constitutively high in transgenic calli due to overexpression but anthocyanin-related genes responded mainly under light
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bag removal followed by continuous white light; light versus dark conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive light-responsive upstream regulator required for full light-induced anthocyanin accumulation in pear
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a functional complex with PpHY5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin-related gene promoters; with PpHY5 strongly enhances activation of PpMYB10 and PpCHS
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation, primarily through cooperation with PpHY5 rather than direct promoter binding to PpMYB10/PpCHS in yeast one-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression constructs in Arabidopsis, pear calli, and subcellular localization assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNA-seq suggested modulation of additional pathways besides flavonoid biosynthesis, including carotenoid biosynthesis and amino acid biosynthesis; no explicit growth penalty reported in pear
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering enhanced red coloration and anthocyanin accumulation in pear, especially in combination with light-responsive regulators such as PpHY5 and the PpMYB10 module
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Empty and PpBBX16-overexpression pear calli under dark/light using Illumina HiSeq X; accession number NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpBBX16 alone could induce promoter activity in dual-luciferase assays but could not directly bind tested promoters in yeast one-hybrid; full function likely requires DNA-binding partner PpHY5. A highly similar homolog, PpBBX16-2, was cloned but was not the main experimentally validated core regulator in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts cooperatively with PpBBX16 to enhance activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes and anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpHY5 physically interacted with PpBBX16 in yeast two-hybrid and BiFC assays. 2. The PpBBX16/PpHY5 complex strongly enhanced activation of PpMYB10 and PpCHS promoters in dual-luciferase assays. 3. PpHY5 likely provides DNA-binding support for PpBBX16 on G-box-containing promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential cooperative partner enabling full PpBBX16 function on anthocyanin gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a functional complex with PpBBX16
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Co-activates PpMYB10 and PpCHS promoters with PpBBX16
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation through cooperative complex formation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive anthocyanin regulatory gene whose expression is induced by light and further activated by the PpBBX16/PpHY5 module
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among tested regulatory genes, PpMYB10 was upregulated by light in pear peel. 2. PpBBX16 enhanced PpMYB10 promoter activity in dual-luciferase assays, and this activation was stronger with PpHY5. 3. PpMYB10 is positioned downstream of PpBBX16/PpHY5 in the proposed model for light-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis during light treatment; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated by light exposure in pear peel
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in response to light and affected by PpBBX16 overexpression/silencing
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key downstream transcriptional regulator in the light-induced anthocyanin module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts downstream to induce anthocyanin structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PpMYB10 promoter used in dual-luciferase assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Established downstream target for improving pear red coloration, potentially in combination with upstream light regulators
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although important in the pathway, the study primarily centered on PpBBX16; PpMYB10 is included because it was directly assayed as a regulatory target in the Results.
